topic: Universities should require students to take courses in climate science

systemArguments.text: Climate change is the most pressing issue of our time and all educated citizens need to understand it
Universities have a responsibility to prepare students for the challenges they will face in their careers
Climate science literacy is essential for informed democratic participation and voting
Many students would not choose to take climate courses without requirements, missing critical knowledge
Climate impacts affect all fields of study and professional practice
Universities already require general education courses in other important areas like mathematics and writing
Climate education promotes critical thinking and scientific literacy skills
Students need to understand climate science to make informed personal and professional decisions
Climate requirements would help combat misinformation and science denial

goldenArguments.text: Universities have a civic responsibility to educate citizens for democratic participation
Climate literacy is essential knowledge for functioning in modern society
Educational institutions should prepare students for the challenges they will face
Scientific literacy requirements promote evidence-based thinking and decision-making
Climate science affects all disciplines and professional fields
General education requirements ensure well-rounded knowledge for all graduates
Climate education combats misinformation and promotes scientific understanding

humanRationale: System arguments show moderate sophistication in addressing educational responsibility, civic preparation, and scientific literacy. They capture several key supporting themes and demonstrate understanding of general education principles. However, they sometimes conflate different justifications (practical vs. civic) and lack deeper philosophical reasoning about the university's role in society and the nature of essential knowledge for citizenship.
